findings.title: Bottom Line is too compressed and omits market value setup
findings.recommendation: Require Bottom Line to read as a 3-5 paragraph executive summary that introduces market value or valuation range before judging valuation.

findings.title: Main report overexposes internal provenance
findings.recommendation: Prohibit saved/deterministic/artifact/local-path/provider-mechanics language in main-body investor sections; move it to Data Issues And Discrepancies, Sources And Evidence, sidecars, or validation artifacts.

findings.title: Business profile and model sections do not explain the business deeply enough
findings.recommendation: Require targeted procedural research when deterministic outputs do not explain products, technology, customers, revenue model, acquisition contribution, and demand drivers in plain language.

findings.title: Market snapshot and valuation sections recite numbers more than they analyze them
findings.recommendation: Require table-based market/financial snapshots plus technical analysis, support/resistance, trend interpretation, valuation range analysis, and explicit discussion of what the market is pricing in.

findings.title: Data quality belongs near the bottom, not in the opening narrative
findings.recommendation: Merge Source Base And Data Quality and Explicit Data Gaps into Data Issues And Discrepancies near the bottom, before Sources And Evidence.

findings.title: Risk section should focus on company/security risk
findings.recommendation: Move data-quality and research-risk discussion out of Risks And Invalidation Points and into Data Issues And Discrepancies.

next_step: Run a separate implementation task using the revised plan, then rerun QUBT as a smoke test and manually review the investor report.
